qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: DataInput parser bypasses configured stream-read constraints and permits denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same UTF8DataInputJsonParser maxNestingDepth bypass on createParser(..., DataInput) that the advisory and fix describe, including the direct createChild*Context calls that skipped the validated nesting-depth helper.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Non-blocking JSON parsers miss the final maxDocumentLength check and accept oversized input
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns async parser maxDocumentLength enforcement on the final feedInput() chunk, which is a different parser family and a different missing check from the advisory's UTF8DataInputJsonParser nesting-depth bypass.

selected_finding.title: DataInput parser bypasses configured stream-read constraints and permits denial of service

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the GHSA's root cause because it targets the same DataInput-backed UTF8DataInputJsonParser branch where maxNestingDepth validation was skipped, and the copied PoC reproduced the advisory's denial-of-service effect.
evidence: The advisory summary names UTF8DataInputJsonParser, parsing from java.io.DataInput, and bypass of maxNestingDepth; the finding names the same parser surface and same missing guard.

The upstream fix referenced by the advisory changes UTF8DataInputJsonParser to call createChildArrayContext()/createChildObjectContext() instead of writing directly to _streamReadContext.createChild*Context(), which is the exact branch the finding cites.

The copied PoC run log shows inputstream-depth=rejected and datainput-depth=accepted under maxNestingDepth=100, proving the advisory-described vulnerable logic was activated only on the DataInput path.
The same run log shows oom-inputstream=rejected and oom-datainput=oom, demonstrating the advisory-described resource-exhaustion consequence from excessive nesting.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC accepted over-depth JSON only on the DataInput parser and then produced oom-datainput=oom under a 256 MB heap while the InputStream parser rejected the same nested payload.
